q: Which type of lenses are used in telescope? (2024)
Which part of the eye functions similarly to the diaphragm in a camera, controlling the amount of light that enters the eye? (2024)
What does monochromatic light refer to? (2024)
a: B. Light of a single wavelength or colour

q: ___________ is the defect of vision in which distant objects are seen clearly, but objects near-by are blurry. (2024)
A microscope uses lenses to make ___________ images of objects. (2024)
What type of lens is used as an eyepiece to produce an erect final image in terrestrial telescope? (2024)
A ___________ controls the time during which light enters the camera through the lens. (2024)
The core of a fibre-optic cable is made of ___________. (2025)
When taking a photograph in bright light a ___________ f-number must be used. (2024)
The yellow spot which is the most light-sensitive spot is ___________. (2025)
a: B. in front of the retina

q: Which part of the human eye is responsible for adjusting the focus of light to form a clear image on the retina? (2025)
Which color of light is more sensitive to the human eye? (2025)
What principle is utilized in a compound microscope to achieve high magnification? (2024)
a: A. Refraction of light through a convex lens

q: What is the SI unit of illuminance? (2025)

A microscope uses lenses to make ___________ images of objects. (2025)
The ___________ which is the colored portion of the eye controls the amount of light energy entering the eye. (2025)
Which of the following is the SI basic unit of luminous intensity? (2025)

The film consists of a thin layer of chemical compounds of ___________. (2025)
___________ is a defect of vision in which near objects are seen clearly, but objects far away are blurry. (2025)
Which wavelength corresponds to the maximum sensitivity of the human eye under day light? (2025)

a: A. To protect the core from external damage
B. To increase the core's refractive index
C. To provide a lower refractive index to cause total internal reflection in the core
